title: Xanthosia tenuior
taxonomy: Xanthosia tenuior is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. It is native to Australia and is often found in coastal areas and sandy soils. The plant is a member of the genus Xanthosia, which consists of several species of flowering plants native to Australia. The genus Xanthosia is part of the larger family Apiaceae, which includes many other species of plants found in Australia and other parts of the world. Xanthosia tenuior is a small, annual or perennial herb with small white or yellow flowers.

intro: Xanthosia tenuior, commonly known as yellow-leaf xanthosia, is a perennial herb belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and reduce the severity of respiratory infections.
